title: Lease vs Buy Car Calculator: Which Is Actually Cheaper?
description: One option drains your net worth 3x faster—and it's not the one you think. Over 5 years, the true cost difference will shock you. See the math.

schemas.name: Lease vs Buy Car Calculator
schemas.description: Interactive calculator to compare the true cost of leasing versus buying a car over 3, 5, and 7 years, factoring in depreciation, interest, maintenance, and insurance.

schemas.headline: Lease vs Buy Car Calculator: Which Is Actually Cheaper?
schemas.description: One option drains your net worth 3x faster—and it's not the one you think. Over 5 years, the true cost difference will shock you. See the math.

mainEntity.name: Is it cheaper to lease or buy a car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Over a short period (3 years), leasing often looks cheaper due to lower monthly payments. However, over 5 to 7 years, buying is almost always cheaper because you eventually own an asset with residual value and eliminate monthly payments.

mainEntity.name: What is a car lease calculator?

acceptedAnswer.text: A car lease calculator estimates your monthly lease payment and total out-of-pocket costs by factoring in the vehicle price, money factor (interest rate), lease term, residual value, down payment, and taxes.

mainEntity.name: How does a lease vs buy car calculator work?

acceptedAnswer.text: It compares the total cost of ownership for both options. For buying, it calculates loan payments, depreciation, and equity. For leasing, it calculates total lease payments and fees. It then compares the net cost after 3, 5, or 7 years.

mainEntity.name: Should I lease a car to get a lower monthly payment?

acceptedAnswer.text: While leasing provides a lower monthly payment, you are continuously paying for the steepest part of a car's depreciation without building any equity. It makes sense for cash flow, but not for long-term wealth building.

mainEntity.name: When does leasing a car make financial sense?

acceptedAnswer.text: Leasing makes sense if you can deduct the lease as a business expense, if you strictly want a new car every 3 years and are willing to pay a premium for it, or if you invest the monthly payment savings into high-yield assets.
